phase_exit_criteria: guest intake → auth merge → L1 letter → mark-sent → audit log

decisions.decision: Two loop systems: product (case-tick/router) vs dev (harness agents). prompts/README.md documents name collisions.
TickTrigger in lib/loops/tick-types.ts to break router↔case-tick circular import
Migrations 001-010 copied from workspace supabase/; agent_role UPPERCASE per SQL canonical
playwright.config.ts opt-in PLAYWRIGHT_CHROME_CHANNEL env drives system Chrome when bundled Playwright binary unavailable; default unchanged for CI/machines with working playwright install

Phase 2 slices 12-18 proposed (Verifier OCR, PDF bundle, bundle/proof-gate integration, Razorpay, rankings cron, ops dashboard expansion, SEO pages). slice-16 (rankings cron) recommended to ship first/standalone since it closes an existing Track A deploy gap (vercel.json already schedules a cron route that does not exist yet).

VERIFIER OCR agent design (slice-12) is blocked pending confirmation of whether NVIDIA MiniMax-M3 supports vision/image input - prompts/product/VERIFIER.md predates the NVIDIA swap and assumes Sonnet-style vision.
No PDF rendering library chosen yet for slice-13 (evidence bundle PDF) - BUILD_SPEC is silent; needs owner approval before adding any new dependency per CLAUDE.md §5.2.

blockers.blocker: Playwright chromium_headless_shell-1169 binary unavailable in this sandbox (download stalls repeatedly); page.goto()-based e2e specs (tests/e2e/guest-intake.spec.ts, slice-11 scope) cannot run here.
blockers.impact: slice-08 verified on typecheck+lint+test:unit+the in-scope mark-sent.spec.ts (request-only, no browser, 2/2 passing)+verify:no-auto-send; the 3 failing tests belong to slice-11 scope and are an environment limitation, not a code defect.
blockers.resolution: Re-run pnpm test:e2e:smoke on a machine/CI with working Playwright browsers before final phase-exit sign-off.
blockers.latest_note: 6th attempt (chromium-headless-shell specific, smaller 79.8MiB download) got further than the bundled chromium installs - download completed and extraction began (libEGL.dylib written) - but still stalled with zero CPU progress afterward. Consistent across both the bundled chromium target and the headless-shell-only target: download always completes, post-download step (extraction/finalization) never does. Likely a sandbox-specific filesystem/subprocess restriction, not a network issue. Needs to be resolved outside this sandbox.
blockers.resolution_note: Playwright bundled chromium/chromium-headless-shell binaries never finish extracting in this sandbox after 7 total attempts (confirmed via DEBUG=pw:install logging: download always completes, then hangs forever at the extracting archive step). Workaround: playwright.config.ts now supports an opt-in PLAYWRIGHT_CHROME_CHANNEL env var that sets use.channel, letting Playwright drive an already-installed system browser (Google Chrome 149, found at /Applications/Google Chrome.app) instead of downloading its own binary. Running PLAYWRIGHT_CHROME_CHANNEL=chrome pnpm test:e2e:smoke passes all 6 tests cleanly. Default behavior (no env var set) is unchanged for CI/other machines with working Playwright installs.

blockers.blocker: scripts/harness/run-slice.sh verify_phase_exit() (line 302) calls run_command_gate via `... >/dev/null || fail` without capturing its echoed exit code through command substitution. run_command_gate ends with `echo "$ec"`, so the function itself always returns bash exit status 0 regardless of the wrapped commands actual exit code — the || fail branch can never trigger.
blockers.impact: --verify-phase-exit prints "Phase 1 exit criteria met" even when pnpm test:e2e:smoke genuinely fails (confirmed: 3 failing tests, exit code 1, yet phase-exit reported success). This is a false-positive in the harness verifier itself, not a product code defect. Contrast with verify_slice() which correctly captures via `ec="$(run_command_gate ...)"`.
blockers.resolution: Fix verify_phase_exit() to capture the gate exit code the same way verify_slice() does, e.g. `ec="$(run_command_gate e2e_smoke "pnpm test:e2e:smoke")"; [[ "$ec" == "0" ]] || fail ...`. Flagged to project owner; not fixed in this pass since scripts/harness/* is harness tooling outside slice 02-11 scope.

blockers.blocker: vercel.json schedules a cron at /api/v1/internal/cron/rankings (30 20 * * * = 02:00 IST) but app/api/v1/internal/cron/rankings/route.ts does not exist in the codebase.
blockers.impact: Would 404 on every nightly invocation if deployed as-is. Confirmed independently by DEVOPS_ENGINEER and BACKEND_ENGINEER specialists.
blockers.resolution: Owner to choose: remove the cron entry from vercel.json until slice-16 ships, or approve a minimal stub route now. See .claude/session/deploy/plan.md §3.
blockers.resolution_note: Owner chose to remove the rankings cron entry (and its functions-block config) from vercel.json rather than stub it. Will restore both when slice-16 (rankings cron) ships.

blockers.blocker: SECURITY_AUDITOR FAIL on slice-12 (Verifier OCR): the cross_border_ai consent text (disclaimers.ts Block F) was written for text/JSON classification and never mentions sending images/scanned documents/ID photos to a third-party AI provider. Confirmed no fuller wording exists anywhere in BUILD_SPEC.md (line 275 only lists the consent_type enum name).
blockers.impact: Sending raw evidence images (pan_card, bank_statement, aadhaar_masked) to NVIDIA for OCR is a materially bigger ask than current consent covers. Blocks slice-12 IMPLEMENTER start.
blockers.resolution: Owner must approve new/amended disclaimer wording or a new consent type before IMPLEMENTER proceeds. Proposed wording presented in chat, awaiting explicit sign-off - not unilaterally decided since Blocks A-H are verbatim-per-spec, not paraphrasable.
